revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:祇園山古墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Gionyama Kofun (祇園山古墳) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kurume City}} | image_caption = | Other_name = | part_of = Gionyama Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Mii, Kurume, Fukuoka|ja|御井町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mii, Kurume, Fukuoka|1=Mii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11488770}}, Kurume City, Fukuoka Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|33.303305555556|N|130.55434166667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Square Kofun (base elliptical) | built = Mid-3rd century | area = 23.7 meters (east-west), 22.9 meters (north-south), height approx. 6 meters | Designated_site = Prefectural Historic Site | Excavated_items = {{ill|Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, modified {{ill|TLV mirror|en|TLV mirror|qid=Q3316102|lt=rectangular-rimmed divine mirrors|1=TLV mirror}}, pottery coffin remains from mound base containing fragments of Han mirrors, jade magatama, jasper beads, iron tools, and other iron weapons and agricultural implements | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|coordinates=33|coordinates=18|coordinates=dd.9|lon_deg=130|lon_min=33|lon_sec=15.63|map_dot_label=Gionyama Kofun|mark=Square mound.svg|marksize=28|caption=location of the site|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} }}
'''Gionyama Kofun''' (祇園山古墳) is a square-shaped {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Mii, Kurume, Fukuoka|ja|御井町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mii, Kurume, Fukuoka|1=Mii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11488770}}, {{ill|Kurume|en|Kurume|qid=Q320934|lt=Kurume|1=Kurume}}, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a {{ill|Prefectural Historic Site|ja|福岡県指定史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Historic Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-3rd century. Some researchers, including {{ill|Hisao Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisao Hōga|1=Toshio Hōga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11452691}}, speculate that this kofun could be the burial site of Himiko, the legendary queen of {{ill|Yamatai|en|Yamatai|qid=Q844295|lt=Yamataikoku|1=Yamatai}}. <ref name=kurume>{{Cite web|url=http://snk.or.jp/cda/bunkazai/mii/bunkazai.html|title=Historic Site of Mii Area|publisher=Kurume City Board of Education|accessdate=2019-dd-02}}</ref> <ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.fsg.pref.fukuoka.jp/bunka/index.asp|title=Fukuoka Prefecture Historic Site Database|publisher=Fukuoka Prefecture|accessdate=2019-dd-02}}</ref>
== Overview ==
Gionyama Kofun is located on the hillside of [[Kōra taisha]], at the edge of a ridge that extends from the western terminus of the {{ill|Mino Mountain Range|ja|耳納山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Mountain Range|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the {{ill|Chikugo Plain|ja|筑後平野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikugo Plain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 筑紫平野|1=Tsukushi Plain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q5367063}}. The site commands a panoramic view of the surrounding plain, suggesting an intentional choice for its location. To the south of the mound, five additional kofun are distributed on the same plateau, collectively known as the "Gionyama Kofun Cluster." <ref name="kurume" /> <ref>{{Cite book|last=Ishiyama|first=Isao|coauthors=Kiyoshi Ōkawa, Akira Takahashi|title=Investigations of Gionyama and Nanamagari Kofun Clusters in Kurume City|publisher=Fukuoka Prefecture Board of Education|year=1979|chapter=Gionyama Kofun Survey|pages=8–dd}}</ref>
The mound was originally situated near the current {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|en|Kyushu Expressway|qid=Q1060803|lt=Kyushu Expressway|1=Kyushu Expressway}}. built of the expressway in the late 1960s threatened the kofun, prompting a significant preservation campaign led by the local government and citizens. Excavations were conducted in five phases between December dd, 1969, and June 1, 1972, revealing the site's historical significance. Roughly 80% of the site was preserved in situ. <ref>{{Cite book|title=Historic Sites of Kurume City|publisher=Kurume City Board of Education|year=2008|page=90|chapter=Gionyama Kofun}}</ref>
== burial and area ==
The kofun is a square-shaped mound, slightly elliptical at the base. It measures approximately 23.7 meters (east-west) by 22.9 meters (north-south) with a height of about 6 meters. The mound's base was carved out of the natural terrain, typeing a terraced type. Surrounding structures include pottery coffins and stone cist burials at the mound's perimeter.
No {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} were found on the site, and the central burial chamber features a box-type Sarcophagus without additional compartments. <ref>{{Cite book|last=Ishiyama|first=Isao|title=Investigations of Gionyama and Nanamagari Kofun Clusters|publisher=Fukuoka Prefecture Board of Education|year=1979}}</ref>
== Associated Burials ==
The surrounding area revealed over 66 burial features, including pottery coffins, stone-lined graves, and vertical stone chambers. These structures, thought to have been constructed over a span of 50 years, suggest that the kofun was a communal burial site associated with a local ruling elite. <ref name="kurume" />
== Speculations about Himiko ==
Some scholars propose that Gionyama Kofun might be Himiko's burial site, citing its map_size, location, and associated burial goods. However, this theory remains a subject of debate. <ref name="kurume" />
== References ==
=== notes === {{Reflist|group=Note}} === Sources === {{Reflist}}
== Related Literature == * {{Cite book|last=Hōga|first=Hisao|title=Supplemental Theories on Himiko's Tomb: Gionyama Kofun|publisher=Kodai no Umi no Kai|year=2001}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
